title: Enable pod restore with crun
body:
`CRRuntimeSupportsPodCheckpointRestore()` is used to check if the current container runtime (e.g., runc or crun) can restore a container into an existing Pod. It does this by processing output message to check if the `--lsm-mount-context` option is supported.  This option was [recently added to crun](https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1578), however, crun and runc have slightly different output messages:

```
$ crun restore --lsm-mount-contextt
restore: option '--lsm-mount-context' requires an argument
Try `restore --help' or `restore --usage' for more information.
```

```
$ runc restore --lsm-mount-context
ERRO[0000] flag needs an argument: -lsm-mount-context
```

This pull request updates the `CRRuntimeSupportsPodCheckpointRestore()` function to support both runtimes.

Sorry I missed this while it was in flight.

As best I can tell, neither [f40](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5068076490686464/html/int-podman-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-out-of-and-into-pod--ipc-net---1) nor [rawhide](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4786601513975808/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-out-of-and-into-pod--net-uts-pid---1) have the necessary crun version yet: the tests which (*I think*) are the ones that should no longer be skipped, are still being skipped.

crun is currently **1.17-1** on all CI systems. REQUEST: someone please insert a comment into this PR detailing which version of crun is expected to have this new feature.

I have added a TODO item to my list, for next time we bump CI VMs, to confirm that "`podman checkpoint and restore container out of and into pod (%s)`" tests do not get skipped. If this warrants an actual issue, please create one and assign it to me.

@edsantiago Well it is in crun main so assuming it will be in crun 1.18
I also a made personal note to confirm this with the next update cycle.
